title: betula platyphylla subsp. kamtschatica
intro: Betula platyphylla subsp. kamtschatica, also known as Kamchatka paper birch, is a subspecies of paper birch tree native to Russia and northeast Asia, characterized by its white papery bark and broad, flat leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Betula platyphylla subsp. kamtschatica include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and improving cardiovascular health. Betula platyphylla subsp. kamtschatica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its bark can also be used to make a salve or ointment for topical application.
